Bank of Korea's 7th consecutive rate freeze at 2.5% was announced Friday (April 10, 2026), citing this March data, renewed energy pressures from Iran conflict, and won volatility—ruling out cuts and likely shifting prediction markets on BoK policy[4]. Core inflation held steady at 2.5% in February[2]. No newer data post-March confirmed as of now[1][7].
